citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
The facility does not have the appropriate facilities, personnel, or equipment to provide adequate veterinary care to an
adult female pig.
An employee noticed blood on the animal's rear on Monday, March 9. On Tuesday, March 10, the attending veterinarian
externally examined the animal in her enclosure. The veterinarian could not perform a rectal or vaginal exam that day due
to the animal's temperament and enclosure size. The facility did not have the resources (such as livestock panels, a
chute, or employee time) to further confine the animal for diagnostics or closer examination. The attending veterinarian
hypothesized that the animal was bleeding from the vagina.
The attending veterinarian recommended transporting the animal to a hospital for a comprehensive exam and for
diagnostics such as imaging. The facility does not have a vehicle that could safely transport the animal and elected to
instead to provide supportive care: move the animal indoors, start a pain medication, and monitor her closely. Since then,
the animal's bleeding has continued and her appetite has decreased. The inspector observed blood staining on the
animal's tail and rear end.
Without resources such as an appropriate vehicle, a livestock chute, or livestock panels, the attending veterinarian has
not been able to diagnose or treat this animal's illness and ensure the provision of adequate veterinary care.
The facility must ensure the availability of appropriate facilities, personnel, equipment, and services to comply with the
provisions of adequate veterinary care for this animal and all other animals that reside at this facility.
Correction date: 10:00 AM on March 18, 2026.
A focused inspection was conducted starting on 3/16/2026. This inspection report is limited to the Direct non-compliance
identified during that inspection. An additional inspection report will be delivered with the other issues identified during this
inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(1)  Direct
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
The facility does not have the appropriate facilities, personnel, or equipment to provide adequate veterinary care to an 
adult female pig.  
 
An employee noticed blood on the animal's rear on Monday, March 9. On Tuesday, March 10, the attending veterinarian 
externally examined the animal in her enclosure. The veterinarian could not perform a rectal or vaginal exam that day due 
to the animal's temperament and enclosure size. The facility did not have the resources (such as livestock panels, a 
chute, or employee time) to further confine the animal for diagnostics or closer examination. The attending veterinarian 
hypothesized that the animal was bleeding from the vagina. 
 
The attending veterinarian recommended transporting the animal to a hospital for a comprehensive exam and for 
diagnostics such as imaging. The facility does not have a vehicle that could safely transport the animal and elected to 
instead to provide supportive care: move the animal indoors, start a pain medication, and monitor her closely. Since then, 
the animal's bleeding has continued and her appetite has decreased. The inspector observed blood staining on the 
animal's tail and rear end.  
 
Without resources such as an appropriate vehicle, a livestock chute, or livestock panels, the attending veterinarian has 
not been able to diagnose or treat this animal's illness and ensure the provision of adequate veterinary care. 
 
The facility must ensure the availability of appropriate facilities, personnel, equipment, and services to comply with the 
provisions of adequate veterinary care for this animal and all other animals that reside at this facility. 
 
Correction date: 10:00 AM on March 18, 2026. 
 
A focused inspection was conducted starting on 3/16/2026. This inspection report is limited to the Direct non-compliance 
identified during that inspection. An additional inspection report will be delivered with the other issues identified during this 
inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
